titleStmt.title: Letter from William Upton Tyrrell to William J.H. Tyrrell 16, August, 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Upton Tyrrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Discusses Gernamy's push for peace. Fresh vegetables are plentiful.

msIdentifier.repository: OPW-Maynooth University Archive & Research Centre at Castletown

correspAction.placeName: Ballindoolin House, Ballindoolin, Edenderry, Kings Co., Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
I had a long epistle from Uncle Rob
3 days ago.
16 August 1917 My dearest Daddy
No news but just a line as usual
to tell you all is well. The weather is splendid again which
is a great blessing. Apparently you have been getting
my letters very irregularly lately. That
was on account of my being away
from the batallion. Isee the old Pope
is talking of a German Peace again.
Germany is apparently working very
hard for peace now & I should say
must be on her last legs. She will
get no peace till she come on her
knees & begs for it. She knows
now that she as not a chance and
has got the wind up badly over this
push. Isee by the official German
wireless that they are preparing the
people for a great defeat.
I met my namesake today. The
international football player. He
is a Lieut. Col in the R.A.M.E and is
near here. Iwas very glad to
get the opportunity of talking to him. Our poor Doctor was killed last
week in the show. We have nothing to do here &
will be some time doing it I think. The people here are very
decent and obli d ging. The book which Min sent came
a few days ago and I was
delighted to get it. Ihave lots of
time for reading here. We get
beautiful plums and tomatoes here
now & any amount of fresh
vegetables. No news but with very best
love to all. Your most affec Son
Willie.
